events.text: Patient is a 52-year-old male of White/Caucasian race with an ECOG performance status of 1
At age 52, patient presented with a large anterior mediastinal mass causing chest discomfort and dry cough, discovered on chest X-ray performed for persistent cough
At age 52, CT-guided core needle biopsy of the mediastinal mass demonstrated diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 52, pathology report showed large lymphoid cells with clear cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli, Ki-67 of 80 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, BCL2, negative for MUM1, CD30 weakly positive, consistent with GCB subtype by Hans algorithm, features suggestive of primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma variant
At age 52, flow cytometry on biopsy material showed monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for CD5, CD23, MUM1
At age 52, FISH analysis showed no MYC rearrangement, no BCL2 rearrangement, no BCL6 rearrangement; gains of 9p24.1 (PD-L1/PD-L2 locus) detected, consistent with primary mediastinal B-cell lymphoma
At age 52, laboratory studies showed LDH elevated at 367 U/L, CBC with WBC 9.8, hemoglobin 14.2 g/dL, platelets 245, CMP within normal limits

At age 52, staging PET/CT demonstrated a large FDG-avid anterior mediastinal mass measuring 10.6 cm, SUVmax 24.8, with compression of the superior vena cava, bilateral supraclavicular lymphadenopathy, no disease below the diaphragm
At age 52, bone marrow biopsy was negative for lymphoma involvement
At age 52, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage II bulky with mediastinal and supraclavicular involvement, no B symptoms, IPI score 2 (low-intermediate risk)
At age 52, patient initiated cycle 1 of R-CHOP at full standard doses with pegfilgrastim support
At age 52, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 1 nausea and grade 1 fatigue, managed conservatively
At age 52, patient received cycle 2 of R-CHOP without complications
At age 52, patient received cycle 3 of R-CHOP on schedule
At age 52, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles showed significant reduction in mediastinal mass to 5.2 cm, SUVmax decreased to 6.8, Deauville score 4, supraclavicular nodes resolved
At age 52, given residual mediastinal FDG uptake at interim assessment with Deauville 4, plan continued to complete 6 cycles of R-CHOP followed by consolidative radiation
At age 52, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of R-CHOP, tolerating well with cumulative grade 1 neuropathy

At age 52, end-of-treatment PET/CT showed further reduction of mediastinal mass to residual soft tissue 2.8 cm with SUVmax 4.1, Deauville score 3, likely representing residual fibrosis versus minimal residual disease
At age 52, patient received consolidative involved-site radiation therapy to the mediastinum, 36 Gy in 18 fractions, completed without significant toxicity
At age 53, PET/CT at 3 months post-radiation showed complete metabolic response with no FDG-avid disease, residual mediastinal soft tissue with SUVmax 2.1, Deauville score 2
At age 53, patient achieved complete remission after combined modality therapy and was transitioned to surveillance
At age 53, surveillance CT at 6 months showed stable residual mediastinal soft tissue consistent with post-treatment fibrosis, no new disease
At age 53, patient reported persistent grade 1 peripheral neuropathy at 12-month follow-up, managed with gabapentin

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 52, pathology report showed large lymphoid cells with clear cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli, Ki-67 of 80 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, BCL2, negative for MUM1, CD30 weakly positive, consistent with GCB subtype by Hans algorithm, features suggestive of primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma variant

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 52, flow cytometry on biopsy material showed monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for CD5, CD23, MUM1

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
